opp.name: Ferrero_S2P
opp.next_step: Ferrero is in active market-exploration to replace SAP Ariba in a phased manner, driven by Ariba's lack of AI/agentic capability. Two touchpoints to date, both event/intro level (not recorded): Wider-audience discovery session, July 2026 – run discovery-led to lock Ariba pain points, quantified impact/metrics, point-solution vs phased-replacement scope, decision criteria, decision process/timeline and budget owner. Then tailored demo on Merlin Intake + ANA + S2P with a phased coexistence-to-replacement roadmap. Multi-thread into IT for SAP ERP integration scoping. - 29-Jun-2026, kickoff with 9 senior leaders – Roberta Bassani (Head of Procurement Digital Hub), Jenna Van Thiel (Global Head of Procurement Excellence), Mattia Sturlese, Mario Baldini, Natacha Rubinovicz, Katarzyna Virassamy, Pierluigi D'Alessio, Macarena Pascual Sebastian, Luis Diaz Lemus. - 02-Jun-2026, Agentic AI in Procurement Roundtable, Luxembourg – Mattia Sturlese (Head of Global Procurement Competence Center), Natacha Rubinovicz (S2PKM Manager), Mario Baldini (Supply Chain Senior Manager).
opp.ais_why: Attended the Luxembourg AI World Tour - Luxembourg and have confirmed they want to replace Ariba in a phased manner due to its lack of AI capabilities. Ferrero’s interest in AI is strong because their team attended the in-person AI World Tour and is specifically interested in ANA and Intake. AI is not just a generic topic here; it appears tied to their broader question of how to modernize procurement beyond their current SAP Ariba setup. Zycus should connect AI to practical outcomes: better guided buying, autonomous tail-spend negotiation, faster sourcing, smarter contract handling, supplier risk visibility, and improved user adoption.

competition.narrative: SAP Ariba is the named competitor, explicitly referenced in Next_Step__c as the incumbent Ferrero is exploring to replace 'in a phased manner, driven by Ariba's lack of AI/agentic capability'. Salesforce Competitors__c confirms SAP Ariba. However, the 29 Jun kickoff was not recorded, so we do not know Ferrero's specific pain points with Ariba, whether other vendors (Coupa, GEP, Ivalua) are in the evaluation, or whether the decision is 'rip and replace' vs 'phased coexistence' (the narrative states phased, but buyer confirmation is missing). The competitive wedge is implied (Merlin/Agentic AI vs Ariba's lack of AI capability), but we have no buyer quote confirming they value this differentiation, no visibility into how Ariba is defending (likely positioning SAP integration and ecosystem lock-in), and no confirmation of whether Ferrero is sole-sourcing Zycus or running a multi-vendor RFP. The discovery session must lock the competitive set, capture the buyer's articulated Ariba pain points (with quotes), and validate that the Merlin/AI narrative resonates as the decisive wedge.

paper_process.narrative: Paper process and contracting mechanics unknown. No Salesforce fields or call evidence document MSA/SOW status, legal review, procurement channel, InfoSec requirements, or ERP/SAP integration formalization. The 13-product scope (eProcurement, eInvoicing, iSupplier) and Ferrero's global CPG profile imply SAP ERP integration will be a formal gate, and the Next_Step__c narrative references multi-threading 'into IT for SAP ERP integration scoping', but no IT stakeholders are mapped in contact roles and no integration deep-dive is scheduled. For a $250k S2P transformation, typical paper-process gates include InfoSec review (data residency, access controls, AI/Merlin data governance), legal contracting (MSA, SOW, redlining), ERP integration scoping (SAP connectors, master data sync, invoice/PO automation), and potentially an SI/partner for implementation (no partner role documented). Current_Contract_Expiration__c is null, so no forcing function from an expiring Ariba contract is visible. The discovery session must map the full contracting timeline and gate owners (legal, IT, InfoSec, finance), and establish whether Ferrero requires an SI (e.g. for SAP integration) or plans direct implementation.

The 13-product scope includes Merlin, Merlin Intake, Agentic AI, and ANA, signaling AI is central to the deal. However, the 29 Jun kickoff was not recorded, so we have no buyer-quoted validation of AI hunger, no visibility into their AI maturity (are they running AI pilots elsewhere, or is this their first AI procurement initiative?), and no confirmation the Merlin narrative resonates.
